element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Keyboard controls with free look, mouse aiming
Engaging enemy creatures in real-time combat
Locating recharge stations, managing vitality resources
Using terminals to access objectives, AI communication, plot advancement
Navigating maze-like tunnels, finding objectives in open areas
Timing movement through crushers, avoiding molten material and coolant
Activating switches, platform sequences requiring speed
Reading enemy positions; interference in magnetic field environments
Adapting movement physics, managing oxygen depletion
Locating pattern buffer devices, long stretches without saves
Optional discovery of hidden plot information and designer messages

typical_relief_triggers: Finding a save point (pattern buffer device)
Moving from maze-like tunnels to open areas
typical_tension_triggers: Oxygen-free environments with limited oxygen supply
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Oscillating between exploration/puzzle-solving (moderate tension) and combat/hazard avoidance (high tension), with plot exposition serving as tension release and narrative pacing mechanism

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Secret terminals hidden in level geometry; optional plot terminals not required for progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Difficult-to-locate secret terminals containing easter egg messages and supplementary plot information

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic movement, combat, and shield/oxygen management in contained environments with clear objectives
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expanded non-linear exploration, precision hazard navigation, and puzzle-solving with increasing environmental complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Integration of low-gravity environments, magnetic field interference, oxygen management, and extended exploration stretches with minimal save points
notable_spikes: Extended maze sections without save points
Magnetic field zones disabling motion sensor

notable_level_patterns.description: Level six exemplifies typical design: combines open combat areas with maze-like tunnel systems requiring extensive exploration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Smells Like Napalm, Tastes Like Chicken (Level 6)

notable_level_patterns.description: Most levels require accessing specific computer terminals to advance; terminals serve as objective markers and narrative delivery
Levels vary completion requirements: some only require reaching endpoint, others demand specific tasks (item retrieval, switch activation, creature extermination, area securing)
Low-gravity, oxygen-free, or magnetic field environments force players to manage oxygen depletion and navigation challenges simultaneously
Sequences combining crushers, pits of molten material/coolant, and locked door puzzles into spatially integrated challenge sequences
